title: Renderless
description: The #[Renderless] attribute skips the rendering phase of Livewire's lifecycle when an action is called, improving performance for actions that don't modify the component's view.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Apply the #[Renderless] attribute to any action method that doesn't need to re-render the component:
The example above uses wire:intersect to call incrementViewCount() when the user scrolls to the bottom. Since #[Renderless] is applied, the view count is logged but the template doesn't re-render—no part of the page is affected.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/post/⚡show.blade.php use Livewire\Attributes\Renderless;use Livewire\Component;use App\Models\Post; new class extends Component { public Post $post;  public function mount(Post $post) { $this->post = $post; }  #[Renderless] public function incrementViewCount() { $this->post->incrementViewCount(); }};

sections.title: #When to use
sections.content:

sections.title: #Alternative approaches
sections.content:
If you need to conditionally skip rendering or prefer not to use attributes, you can call skipRender() directly in your action:
You can also skip rendering directly from the element using the .renderless modifier:
This approach is useful for one-off cases where you don't want to add an attribute to the method.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/post/⚡show.blade.php use Livewire\Component;use App\Models\Post; new class extends Component { public Post $post;  public function incrementViewCount() { $this->post->incrementViewCount();  $this->skipRender(); }};

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content: For more information about actions and performance optimization, see the Actions documentation.
